summary: End-to-end testing of Marlinda's Mod Skincare site. Backend: 8/8 pytest tests passed against /api/health and /api/contact (validation, persistence, list, no _id leak, email_sent=false). Frontend: hero, sticky nav, smooth-scroll links, mobile drawer (open/close), 13 service cards + modal with correct booker link, 6 team cards, 3 FAQ items toggle, animated reviews counters (5.0/4.7) appear, floating Book Now after scroll, 7-day hours, address, tel/mailto/iframe map, contact form happy-path (success message 'Thank you — we'll be in touch soon.') and required-field native validation all functional. Booker (8) and gift certificate (1) links wired correctly.

minor.issue: logger referenced before defined: in server.py the `except Exception` branch (line 102) and the else branch (line 105) call logger.error/warning, but `logger` is only defined at line 138. If RESEND_API_KEY is set and send fails, this raises NameError. Not triggered in test env (RESEND_API_KEY empty path falls through to logger.warning at line 105 which would also NameError). Move the logging.basicConfig + logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) block above the route handlers.

issues: At default viewport the page-loader cream overlay is fine, but before scrolling the nav header initial state shows a light translucent backdrop while the hero photo is darker — top nav links can have low contrast on the photo. Consider stronger drop shadow or solid bg until scrolled.
Decorative description text appears very low-contrast (cream/grey on cream background) — 'Browse our full menu of services...' is barely readable. Verify contrast meets WCAG AA.

action_items: Move `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` (and logging.basicConfig) above the /api/contact handler in /app/backend/server.py to fix NameError when RESEND_API_KEY path executes.
Spec states 12 services but /app/frontend/src/data/services.js contains 13 entries. Confirm intent and either remove one or update spec.
Improve text contrast in BookExperience subcopy and on the pre-scroll nav over the hero image.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger usage precedes logger initialization (lines 102/105 vs 138). High-risk latent bug in prod.
services.js has 13 cards (spec said 12) — minor product/spec mismatch.
Frontend page-loader fades after ~1.7s as designed; hero z-0 fix verified visually.
seed_data_creation: Two contact submissions persisted with TEST_ prefix via API tests + one TEST_PW_FE submission via Playwright. Safe to delete by email prefix TEST_.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_marlindas_api.py runs in <1s. Frontend public URL is REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env (https://luxury-skincare-spa-1.preview.emergentagent.com). Page loader needs ~2-2.5s wait before interacting. Form testids: contact-input-name|email|phone|message + contact-submit + contact-success. Mobile toggles: nav-mobile-toggle / nav-drawer / nav-drawer-close. FAQ: diamondglow-faq-{0..2}. Service cards: service-card-{id}. Team cards: team-card-{id}. RESEND_API_KEY is intentionally empty — email_sent must be false.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues found. The minor backend logger-ordering bug doesn't manifest under the empty-RESEND_API_KEY test path because Python only resolves names at call time and the offending log lines were not reached when emails skipped — actually correction: the warning at line 105 IS reached when RESEND_API_KEY is empty; however my live calls succeeded, suggesting either the warning is suppressed or the line was hit but didn't crash the request. Re-checked: line 105 is `else: logger.warning(...)` and IS executed. Tests passed regardless, so logger may have been defined by module import side-effects. Still recommend reordering for clarity and safety.
